faqs.question: Can a family member be paid to provide home care in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Yes. Under Alabama's Medicaid self-directed or consumer-directed waiver options, adult children, spouses in some cases, and other relatives can be paid as caregivers. VA programs such as the Program of Comprehensive Assistance for Family Caregivers (PCAFC) may also pay family caregivers of eligible veterans.

faqs.question: How many hours of home care can a senior get through Alabama Medicaid Agency?
faqs.answer: Hours depend on the waiver, the individualized care plan, and the senior's level of care. Some Alabama seniors receive 10–20 hours a week; others with complex needs receive substantially more or qualify for the PACE program.

faqs.question: How is home care different from home health care in New York?
faqs.answer: Home care (often $30/hr in New York) covers personal care, homemaker services, and companionship — typically private-pay. Home health care is clinical (nursing, physical therapy, wound care) and is often covered by Medicare Part A or B following a qualifying hospital stay.

faqs.question: How many hours of home care can a senior get through New York Medicaid?
faqs.answer: Hours depend on the waiver, the individualized care plan, and the senior's level of care. Some New York seniors receive 10–20 hours a week; others with complex needs receive substantially more or qualify for the PACE program.
